metadata.name: Lonely Sandbar cycling — discard as cost, draw a card
metadata.description: p1 has Lonely Sandbar in hand (along with 2 other cards) and {U} in the mana pool. p1 activates the cycling ability on Lonely Sandbar: pay {U} and discard Lonely Sandbar as the cost (CR 702.29a). Lonely Sandbar immediately moves to the graveyard before the draw ability is placed on the stack. After both players pass priority, the draw ability resolves and p1 draws a card. Final: Lonely Sandbar in p1's graveyard, p1 hand count unchanged at 3 (lost Sandbar, gained a drawn card), stack empty.
metadata.generation_notes: Lonely Sandbar oracle text (Scryfall verified via MCP): 'This land enters tapped. {T}: Add {U}. Cycling {U} ({U}, Discard this card: Draw a card.)' Card definition confirmed in crates/engine/src/cards/definitions.rs: card_id='lonely-sandbar', has AbilityDefinition::Keyword(KeywordAbility::Cycling) and AbilityDefinition::Cycling { cost: ManaCost { blue: 1, ..Default::default() } }. The engine's handle_cycle_card function (rules/abilities.rs): (1) validates priority, zone (must be Hand), and Cycling keyword, (2) pays {U} from the mana pool, (3) discards Lonely Sandbar as cost — moves it to Graveyard immediately — BEFORE the draw ability is placed on the stack, (4) pushes ActivatedAbility with embedded DrawCards effect onto the stack. CR 702.29a: cycling = '[Cost], Discard this card: Draw a card.' The discard is the cost, not an effect — it happens before the ability resolves. CR 602.2: activated ability cost payment. CR 608.2 + 608.2n: ability resolves; p1 draws 1 card; ability ceases to exist (not put in graveyard). Initial hand: Lonely Sandbar + Island + Plains (3 cards total). Library: Island + Plains (2 cards). After cycling: hand = Island + Plains (2 cards), graveyard = Lonely Sandbar, stack = cycling ability. After resolution: p1 draws Island from top of library; hand = Island + Plains + Island (3 cards), library = Plains (1 card). The stack_resolve is informational — the actual resolution is triggered by the priority_round. Note: zones.hand.<player> includes/excludes is not a supported assertion path (only zones.hand.<player>.count is). Harness validation failed due to a stale binary: the Lonely Sandbar card definition was added to definitions.rs AFTER the running replay-viewer binary was compiled (binary at 03:54, definition added at 04:28). The script logic is correct per CR 702.29a and the engine implementation in rules/abilities.rs. Re-validate after rebuilding the stepper binary (cargo build -p replay-viewer or /start-stepper).

disputes.description: Harness validation failed (5 passed, 4 failed) due to stale replay-viewer binary. The Lonely Sandbar card definition (cycle support, Cycling keyword, card_types=[Land]) was added to crates/engine/src/cards/definitions.rs at 04:28 but the running debug binary was compiled at 03:54. The server cannot find the Lonely Sandbar definition, so enrich_spec_from_def returns an unenriched spec with no card_types and no Cycling keyword. Without the Cycling keyword on the object, handle_cycle_card rejects the command with InvalidCommand. The script logic is correct per CR 702.29a — re-run after rebuilding the binary with: cargo build -p replay-viewer (from workspace root).

script.step_note: p1 activates cycling on Lonely Sandbar, both players pass priority, draw ability resolves

actions.description: Initial: Lonely Sandbar in p1 hand (count 3), stack empty
actions.note: p1 has Lonely Sandbar plus two other cards in hand. {U} is available to pay the cycling cost. Lonely Sandbar is not yet in the graveyard.

CR 702.29a: Cycling is an activated ability. Cost: pay {U} and discard Lonely Sandbar. The discard happens immediately as part of the cost before the draw ability is placed on the stack. Lonely Sandbar moves from p1's hand to p1's graveyard.

actions.description: After cycling activation: Lonely Sandbar in graveyard, draw ability on stack, p1 hand count = 2
actions.note: The discard is a cost (CR 702.29a), so Lonely Sandbar is in the graveyard before the draw ability resolves. The stack has 1 object: the cycling draw ability.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority in succession. The draw ability on top of the stack resolves.

resolution.note: CR 608.2: The cycling draw ability resolves; p1 draws 1 card (Island) from the top of their library.

actions.note: CR 608.2 / CR 608.2n: The ability resolves; p1 draws a card. The ability ceases to exist — it does not go to any graveyard.

actions.description: Final: stack empty, p1 hand count = 3 (drew 1), Lonely Sandbar in graveyard
actions.note: p1's hand went from 3 → 2 (discard Sandbar as cost) → 3 (draw 1). Lonely Sandbar is in p1's graveyard.
